title: IDF and Shin Bet Kill Iyad Shambari, Hamas Military Intelligence Operations Chief Linked to October 7 Planning
detail: The IDF and Shin Bet jointly announced on April 29, 2026 that an Israeli airstrike in the al-Rimal neighborhood of northern Gaza City had killed Iyad Ahmed Abd al-Rahman Shambari, the head of the Operations Department within Hamas's Military Intelligence Headquarters. Shambari was assessed as having 'taken an active part' in planning the October 7, 2023 massacre and was responsible for compiling Hamas's operational situational assessments of the entire Gaza Strip to direct attack plans against Israeli soldiers and communities. The military described him as a 'key figure' in Hamas's intelligence-gathering apparatus who posed an immediate threat to IDF forces operating in Gaza. Gaza's civil defence agency reported that the strike in al-Rimal killed at least four people and wounded six others. The killing continued a pattern of near-daily Israeli targeted strikes against senior Hamas commanders and confirmed October 7 perpetrators during the nominal October 2025 ceasefire — the IDF has previously confirmed kills of Hazem Rami Ali Aidi (April 26), Khamis Muhammad Khamis Qassas (April 21), and multiple other October 7 participants in 2026. Hamas had not publicly commented on the claim at time of initial reporting.

media.caption: IDF and Shin Bet confirm killing of Iyad Shambari, Hamas military intelligence chief linked to October 7 planning, April 29, 2026

title: Phase Two Disarmament Deadlock Deepens on Day 202 as Hamas Maintains Refusal; UN Security Council Urges Progress
detail: As of April 29, 2026 — Day 202 of the October 10, 2025 ceasefire — the Gaza Phase Two disarmament negotiations remain in complete deadlock with no signs of breakthrough. Hamas has maintained its categorical rejection of the Board of Peace's eight-month disarmament framework, which calls for Hamas to surrender rockets, drones, anti-tank missiles, and tunnel maps within 90 days. Hamas has demanded a full Israeli withdrawal from Gaza and a guaranteed pathway to Palestinian statehood as preconditions for any disarmament discussion — conditions the Netanyahu government categorically rejects. All four Board of Peace deadlines (April 11, April 14, April 19, and the informal April 25–28 window) have passed without Hamas acceptance. The Foundation for Defense of Democracies assessed on April 28 that Hamas's continued refusal sets the stage for potential Gaza escalation if no new diplomatic formula emerges. The UN Security Council's April 28 briefing by ASG Khiari renewed calls for both sides to implement Phase One obligations and accelerate Phase Two, warning that the 'status quo cannot hold indefinitely.' Meanwhile, the Board of Peace continued backchannel contacts through Cairo and Ankara mediators, with Qatar and Egypt serving as intermediaries.

media.caption: Phase Two disarmament negotiations at complete impasse as Hamas maintains categorical rejection, April 29, 2026
